title: Network Layer
description: Routes packets across networks using logical addressing (IP).
fullExplanation: The Network Layer is responsible for routing packets from source to destination across multiple networks. It uses logical addressing (IP addresses) and routing protocols (BGP, OSPF, RIP) to determine the best path. Routers operate at this layer. IPv4 uses 32-bit addresses (4.3 billion), IPv6 uses 128-bit addresses (340 undecillion). This layer handles fragmentation, reassembly, and TTL (Time To Live).
keyFacts: BGP routes internet traffic between ISPs
